module.description: Defines a hardware design unit encapsulating combinational and sequential logic with input/output ports, parameters, and internal signal declarations for synthesizable RTL design.

interface.description: Defines a bundle of signals and optional protocol logic that can be shared between modules, reducing port-level connections and enabling modular communication protocol definitions.

class.description: Defines an object-oriented class for verification with properties, methods, constructor, constraints, and inheritance support, primarily used in testbench and UVM environments.

new.description: Constructor method called when creating a new instance of the class.

randomize.description: Generates random values for rand/randc variables subject to defined constraints.

logic.description: Four-state data type (0, 1, x, z) that can be used in both procedural assignments and continuous assignments, replacing the need to choose between reg and wire in Verilog.

wire.description: Net type representing a physical connection driven by continuous assignments or module port connections, resolving multiple drivers according to resolution functions in hardware modeling.

reg.description: Variable type that holds its value between procedural assignments in always blocks, representing storage elements in sequential logic though not necessarily synthesized as registers.

always_ff.signature: always_ff @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin ... end
always_ff.description: Procedural block specifically for modeling flip-flop based sequential logic, enforced by synthesis tools to contain only synchronous assignments with clock and optional reset sensitivity.

always_comb.description: Procedural block for modeling combinational logic that automatically infers a complete sensitivity list from all signals read within the block, preventing unintended latch inference.

always_latch.description: Procedural block explicitly intended for latch-based logic, with automatic sensitivity list inference and tool checks ensuring the block correctly implements level-sensitive storage elements.

constraint.description: Defines declarative constraints on random variables within a class, specifying valid value ranges, relationships, distributions, and conditional rules for constrained random verification.

covergroup.signature: covergroup name @(event); coverpoint var { bins name = {values}; } endgroup
covergroup.description: Defines a functional coverage model with coverpoints and cross coverage for tracking which design scenarios have been exercised during simulation verification campaigns.

assertion.signature: assert property (@(posedge clk) antecedent |-> consequent) else $error("msg")
assertion.description: SystemVerilog assertion that formally specifies and checks temporal properties of the design during simulation, using sequences and properties for protocol and timing verification.

typedef.signature: typedef data_type new_type_name | typedef enum {labels} enum_type
typedef.description: Creates a user-defined type alias or enumerated type for improved code readability, type safety, and abstraction of complex data type declarations in hardware designs.

struct.signature: typedef struct [packed] { type1 field1; type2 field2; ... } struct_name
struct.description: Groups related signals into a composite data type with named fields, with the packed qualifier ensuring contiguous bit-level representation for synthesis and bus-width calculations.
